{

    "Basar T, Olsder GJ 1998 - The book explicitly connects game theory (including principal-agent problems) and H-infinity control, framing both as minimax problems under uncertainty - Book",
    "James MR, Baras JS, Elliott RJ 1994 - Formulates robust control problems using risk-sensitive criteria, which are mathematically linked to relative entropy and have strong parallels to utility maximization under uncertainty in economics - Journal Article (IEEE Transactions on Automatic Control)",
    "Hansen LP, Sargent TJ 2008 - This seminal work on robust macroeconomics explicitly imports robust control (H-infinity) techniques to model decision-making under model uncertainty, directly analogizing the 'evil nature' player to worst-case disturbances - Book",
    "Zhou K, Doyle JC, Glover K 1996 - While a control theory textbook, it lays the game-theoretic foundations of H-infinity control (minimax two-player zero-sum differential games), which is the mathematical bridge to mechanism design's saddle-point structure - Book"

}
